reasoning: Based on the FRED WLCFLL series, BTFP arbitrage loans completely rolled off by April 2025, leaving a regular discount window base of around $6,500m. The current value as of Nov 5 (published Nov 6 and valid for the Nov 9 baseline) is 7230. This value is somewhat higher than the recent mid-month averages (~6100-6600). Since Target 1 (Nov 16) will capture the Nov 12 mid-month reading, historical patterns strongly suggest normal discount window usage dips mid-month, so the probability of exceeding 7230 is low. Target 2 (Dec 9) will capture the early December reading (Dec 3) right after month-end and Thanksgiving, where temporary liquidity frictions can cause spikes, making it closer to a coin toss but still facing a high hurdle of 7230. For Target 3 (Feb 2026), the further horizon and possible slow upward drift of discount window usage due to continued balance-sheet normalization pushes the probability closer to 50%.
